title: Size Select: Persistence
pitch: A follow-up test after the compact grid was established as the winner. Returning customers already know their size, so asking them to re-select it on every product page is unnecessary friction. This experience remembers the customer's size per gender category and auto-selects it on subsequent visits, with contextual messaging like "We have your size in stock!"
problem: Returning customers had to re-select their size on every PDP visit, even though they already knew it. This added a repetitive step to every product browse.
hypothesis: Persisting size selection per gender category and auto-selecting on return visits would reduce friction and accelerate the path to purchase for returning customers.
